channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Firefox and Windows zero-day security bugs hit by Russian hackers, so be on your guard
item.description: Russian hackers seen exploiting two zero-days to deploy backdoors against victims in Europe and North America.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple's Next iPad Pro: M5 Chipset Upgrade and OLED Display Hints at 2025 Release
item.description: Apple's next iPad Pro, powered by an M5 chipset and featuring an OLED display, might be released in 2025. The upgrade is rumored to arrive before the end of the year.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Australia's social media ban for kids is closer to becoming law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia’s House of Representatives on Wednesday passed a bill that would ban children younger than 16 years old from social media, leaving it to the Senate to finalize the world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Windows Recall: All your privacy questions answered
item.description: Windows Recall is an AI-based feature that will be built into new Copilot+ PCs. Recall takes snapshots of your screen every few seconds and uses that data to make a searchable index of everything you’ve ever done on your PC.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Python to C: What’s new in Cython 3.1
item.description: Cython is the most convenient way to bridge C and Python languages and tools in Python programs. Developers use it to write C extensions or wrap C libraries for use in Python. Now, a major new release is on the way.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Brico Depôt lleva a cabo una total transformación de la compañía gracias a la digitalización
item.description: El auge cada vez mayor de la tendencia ‘do it yourself‘ (DIY) y el creciente interés de los usuarios por renovar su hogar ha propiciado un exponencial crecimiento del mercado del bricolaje en los últimos años.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Australian father of teen sextortion victim backs banning young children from social media
item.description: Wayne Holdsworth became an advocate for banning Australian children younger than 16 from social media because his son took his own life after falling victim to an online sextortion scam.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Federal prosecutors seek records from company that deployed AI weapons scanner on NYC subway
item.description: Federal investigators in New York have requested records from the manufacturer of an AI-powered weapons scanner that was briefly deployed in New York City’s subway system.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Shining a light on shadow AI: Three ways to keep your enterprise safe
item.description: To mitigate shadow AI, leaders should build cross-functional teams to leverage interdisciplinary expertise and foster organizational awareness. The post Shining a light on shadow AI: Three ways to keep your enterprise safe first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: How to (kindly) ask people not to post your kids' photos on social media
item.description: With kids facing a seemingly increasing amount of threats from creeps on the internet, here's how you can respectfully ask people not to post photos of your children on social media.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Explicit ‘deepfake' images of nearly 50 teens rocks Pennsylvania school
item.description: Students have used AI technology to create fake explicit images of classmates in Washington, California and New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Global Internet Outages on the Rise: ThousandEyes Reports
item.description: Internet outages, ISP outages, cloud provider network outages, and collaboration app network outages reported by ThousandEyes.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: These digital product trends will define 2025
item.description: Consumer preferences, technology innovations, and emerging market forces—from AI capabilities to platform shifts and sustainability imperatives—shape how we build digital products and their success in the market.
item.dc:creator: Brad Weber, Fast Company

item.title: Ask the CIO: Defense Commissary Agency
item.description: Learn how agencies are advancing their cybersecurity posture in today’s complex threat landscape. The post Ask the CIO: Defense Commissary Agency first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Tesla excluded from EV buyer credits in Gov. Newsom’s proposal
item.description: Excluding Tesla could burnish Newsom’s standing on the left as he renews a clash with Musk, who has become a member of Trump’s inner circle and accepted a role helping the incoming administration cut government spending.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Interpol clamps down on cybercrime and arrests over 1,000 suspects in Africa
item.description: Interpol says it arrested 1,006 suspects in Africa during a massive two-month operation, clamping down on cybercrime that left tens of thousands of victims, including some who were trafficked, and produced millions in financial damages.
item.dc:creator: Mark Banchereau, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The biggest IT threat? That seemingly innocuous web browser
item.description: For decades, enterprises have allowed their workers to use whatever free browser they wanted to access the most sensitive files possible. CIOs believed that security software in the environment — such as endpoint security apps or supposedly secure web gateways — would deliver any needed protections.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Accelerating Enterprise Generative AI Development
item.description: Gencore AI empowers organizations to build scalable generative AI applications while ensuring security and compliance.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The engine of Japan's flagship new small rocket explodes during a test for a second time
item.description: The engine for a flagship new small Japanese rocket has burst into flames during a combustion test, but there was no injury or damage to the outside, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google, DOJ wrap up second antitrust trial
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) and Google wrapped up the tech giant’s second antitrust trial in two years Monday, as the company seeks to fend off monopoly allegations in the advertising technology space. After a relatively brief two-week trial in September, the two sides reconvened in U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Free travel planner? Use AI for your next trip
item.description: I’m going to Europe soon, and planning my trip was a breeze. Yes, really! AI did a big chunk of the work for me — and it can help you plan your next getaway, too.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PHP updates DOM API
item.description: PHP 8.4.1, an update to the longstanding server-side web scripting language, has been released with features including an updated DOM API that makes it easier to work with HTML5 documents. Accessible from php.net, PHP 8.4.1 was released November 21.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Is Outlook down? Thousands of Microsoft 365 users report outage issues
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Thousands of Microsoft 365 customers worldwide reported having issues with services like Outlook and Teams on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Elon Musk entertains idea of buying MSNBC
item.description: Elon Musk is flirting with the idea of buying MSNBC amid speculation the left-leaning cable news network could be put up for sale.
item.dc:creator: Brian Niemietz, The Mercury News

item.title: Meet the USC student rocket club that shattered a world altitude record
item.description: This could not be happening. Five years after becoming the first students to launch a rocket into space, the University of Southern California Rocket Propulsion Lab (USCRPL) was readying a redesigned model, Aftershock II, for an even higher ascent from Nevada’s Black Rock Desert.
item.dc:creator: Susan Karlin, Fast Company

item.title: Most young knowledge workers using AI: Poll
item.description: The vast majority of Generation Z knowledge workers said they are using artificial intelligence (AI) tools in their work, according to a new study, underscoring the increased embrace of the advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: These 4 fantastic sites offer more than 17,000 free online courses
item.description: The kids are back in school, the year is winding down, and maybe—just maybe—you’re finding yourself with a teeny, tiny bit of extra time on your hands.
item.dc:creator: Doug Aamoth, Fast Company

item.title: Stanford Professor Accused of Using AI in Court Testimony
item.description: Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison's expert witness accused of using artificial intelligence to craft testimony
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Don’t get caught in the 'Apple ID suspended' phishing scam
item.description: Even the most tech-savvy computer users have to think twice with some of the clever phishing emails scammers are coming up with these days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Free email services are costing you more than you think
item.description: Free email accounts come with hidden costs to privacy. Technology guru Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explores the risks involved with email accounts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Mr. Miyagi's dramatic return
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bitcoin ticks closer to $100,000 in extended surge following US elections
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Bitcoin extended its streak of record highs after ticking above $99,000 for the first time. The cryptocurrency has rocketed more than 40% in just two weeks.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Supreme Court allows multibillion-dollar class action to proceed against Meta
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court is allowing a multibillion-dollar class action investors’ lawsuit to proceed against Facebook parent Meta, stemming from the privacy scandal involving the Cambridge Analytica political consulting firm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: OpenAI is thinking about building its own browser
item.description: OpenAI is reportedly thinking about developing its own browser with the aim of challenging Google’s dominance in the market, according to The Information. The new browser would have built-in support for Chat GPT and Open AI’s search engine Search GPT.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: NetBox Labs launches tools to combat network configuration drift
item.description: NetBox Labs is expanding its network management platform this week with a pair of new products designed to tackle the growing challenges of infrastructure documentation and configuration management.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI agents are unlike any technology ever
item.description: The agents are coming, and they represent a fundamental shift in the role artificial intelligence plays in businesses, governments, and our lives. The biggest news in agentic AI happened this month when we learned that OpenAI’s agent, Operator, is expected to launch in January.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Thai court dismisses activist's suit against Israeli spyware producer over lack of evidence
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A Thai court on Thursday threw out a lawsuit brought by a pro-democracy activist which alleged spyware produced by an Israeli tech firm had been used to hack his phone.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Backup Software vs. Backup as a Service (BaaS): The Key Differences
item.description: As data volumes increase exponentially, the threat of data loss from cyberattacks, human error, or system failure has never been greater — which is why, in 2025, fortifying a data protection strategy has never made more sense.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Red Hat Linux to be official WSL distro
item.description: Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) is set to become an official Linux distribution for Microsoft’s Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL), a Windows feature that allows users to run Linux environments on Windows without a separate virtual machine.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Aviz Networks Secures $17 Million in Funding for Open Networking Solutions and AI Capabilities
item.description: Aviz Networks announces a $17 million Series A funding round to accelerate its growth in open networking solutions and artificial intelligence capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Advanced programming with Java generics
item.description: Generics in Java enhance the type safety of your code and make it easier to read. In my last article, I introduced the general concepts of generics and showed examples from the Java Collections Framework. You also learned how to use generics to avoid runtime errors like the ClassCastException.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia is Wall Street's most valuable company. How it got there, by the numbers
item.description: Nvidia has once again turned out quarterly results that exceeded Wall Street's forecasts. The company has seen soaring demand for its semiconductors, which are used to power artificial intelligence applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5
